sn35.63:1.2: Then Ven. Migajāla went to the Blessed One and on arrival, having bowed down to him, sat to one side. As he was sitting there
sn35.63:1.3: he said to the Blessed One:
sn35.63:1.4: “‘A person who is living alone. A person who is living alone,’ thus it is said.
sn35.63:1.5: To what extent, lord, is one a person who is living alone, and to what extent is one a person who is living with a companion?”
sn35.63:2.1: “Migajāla, there are forms cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire—
sn35.63:2.2: and a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them.
sn35.63:2.3: As he relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them, delight arises.
sn35.63:2.4: There being delight, he is impassioned.
sn35.63:2.6: A monk joined with the fetter of delight is said to be a person who is living with a companion.
sn35.63:2.7: “There are sounds cognizable via the ear… aromas cognizable via the nose… flavors cognizable via the tongue… tactile sensations cognizable via the body…
sn35.63:2.8: ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire—
sn35.63:2.9: and a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them.
sn35.63:2.10: As he relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them, delight arises.
sn35.63:2.11: There being delight, he is impassioned.
sn35.63:2.13: A monk joined with the fetter of delight is said to be a person who is living with a companion.
sn35.63:2.14: “A person living in this way—even if he frequents isolated forest & wilderness dwellings, with an unpopulated atmosphere, lying far from humanity, appropriate for seclusion—is still said to be living with a companion.
sn35.63:2.17: Because the craving that is his companion has not been abandoned by him.
sn35.63:2.18: Thus he is said to be a person who is living with a companion.
sn35.63:3.1: “Now, there are forms cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire—
sn35.63:3.2: and a monk does not relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them. As he doesn’t relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them,
sn35.63:3.4: There being no delight, he is not impassioned.
sn35.63:3.5: Being not impassioned, he is not fettered.
sn35.63:3.6: A monk disjoined from the fetter of delight is said to be a person who is living alone.
sn35.63:3.7: “There are sounds cognizable via the ear… aromas cognizable via the nose… flavors cognizable via the tongue… tactile sensations cognizable via the body…
sn35.63:3.8: ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire—
sn35.63:3.9: and a monk does not relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them. As he doesn’t relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them,
sn35.63:3.11: There being no delight, he is not impassioned.
sn35.63:3.12: Being not impassioned, he is not fettered.
sn35.63:3.13: A monk disjoined from the fetter of delight is said to be a person who is living alone.
sn35.63:3.14: “A person living in this way—even if he lives near a village, associating with monks & nuns, with male & female lay followers, with kings & royal ministers, with sectarians & their disciples—is still said to be living alone. A person living alone is said to be a monk.
sn35.63:3.17: Because the craving that is his companion has been abandoned by him.
sn35.63:3.18: Thus he is said to be a person who is living alone.”
